summary: The primary criterion involves ensuring an hourly urine output greater than 5 mL or daily minimums above 200 mL over two consecutive days without fluid overload concerns; other factors like resolution of symptoms also play crucial roles but are secondary compared to these specific parameters related directly back to circulatory stability and kidney function recovery after severe dengue events leading into potential SHS episodes. To ensure comprehensive safety during transition out-of-hospital settings where close medical supervision may not always be available immediately upon release due to varying levels of severity among cases recovering from such acute conditions necessitating strict adherence towards established guidelines focusing primarily around objective physiological markers rather than subjective symptom
